mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the effective federal funds rate over a three-month period signals an aggressive monetary tightening cycle. This raises the risk-free rate, increases corporate borrowing costs, and reduces the present value of future earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FOMC statements in early 2022 indicated a hawkish pivot and accelerated rate hikes.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the 3-month change in FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.50 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained low levels of the unemployment rate indicate a tight labor market, which fuels wage inflation and prompts the central bank to maintain or increase restrictive monetary policy. This expectation of higher rates for longer negatively impacts equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% in May 2022 is near historic lows, a focus of Fed commentary on inflation.
failure_condition: A rolling 3-year correlation between the level of UNRATE and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive and statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing growth in real retail sales, calculated by deflating nominal sales (RSAFS) by consumer prices (CPIAUCSL), indicates weakening consumer purchasing power and demand. This directly implies lower future corporate revenues and earnings, leading to negative revisions in equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation in 2022 is eroding nominal wage and sales gains.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the signal (e.g., 3-month change in RSAFS/CPIAUCSL) for predicting 20-day forward returns is not statistically different from zero over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year) significantly exceeds the policy rate (FEDFUNDS), the resulting deeply negative real interest rate forces the central bank to tighten policy aggressively. This forward guidance on future rate hikes weighs on equity markets.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY % change > 5% AND FEDFUNDS < (CPIAUCSL YoY % change - 2%)
evidence_refs: fact:As of May 2022, CPI YoY is over 8% while FEDFUNDS is below 1%, creating a large negative real rate.
failure_condition: In periods where CPI YoY is high and real rates are negative, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production, measured by its 3-month rate of change, reflects a robust supply side and growing corporate output. This translates into higher expected earnings and supports positive equity market returns over a short-term horizon.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is consistently below 0.02 on a rolling 3-year basis.

mechanism_narrative: Following an economic downturn, a strong rebound in nonfarm payrolls signals a robust labor market recovery. This increases aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive equity returns as the economy exits recession.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE has peaked and declined for at least 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2008 and post-2020 recoveries showed strong equity performance alongside payrolls growth.
failure_condition: In the 24 months following a peak in the unemployment rate, the correlation between monthly PAYEMS changes and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative or statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in housing starts signals a sharp contraction in the interest-rate sensitive residential construction sector, a reliable leading indicator of a broader economic recession. This negative outlook for aggregate demand leads to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing sector slowdowns preceded the 2001 and 2008 recessions.
failure_condition: Following a month where HOUST drops more than 10%, the S&P 500's average 60-day forward return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A higher-than-expected monthly inflation print forces markets to immediately price in a more hawkish Federal Reserve response. This repricing of the discount rate causes a swift, negative impact on equity prices over a very short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Market reactions to high CPI prints in 2022 have been sharply negative on release days.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following the release of a monthly CPI report showing >0.8% m/m inflation is non-negative.
